chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-1244 of 2019 Const. Petition No.D-69 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. For Directions 1.For orders on CMA 289/24 2.For hearing of CMA 290/24 3.For hearing of CMA 6267/23 4.For hearing of CMA 5798/23 01-10-2024 Petitioner Dr. Naila Parveen present in person. Mr. Niaz Hussain Maitlo, Advocate for respondents-university. Mr. Sohail Ahmed Khoso, Advocate for respondent No.7 in C.P.No.D-69 of 2024 a/w said respondent. Mr. Nisar Ahmed Bhanbhro, Advocate for respondent No.4 in C.P.No.D- 69 of 2024. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant A.G-Sindh. Mr. Ishrat Ali Mirani, Assistant Professor/Member, Syndicate, SALU is present in person. ***** Mr. Niaz Hussain Maitlo, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of alleged contemnors in C.P.No.1244 of 219, taken on record. Comments filed by respondent No.7 in C.P.No.D-69 of 2024 are taken on record and copy supplied to the petitioner. A report of Prof. Dr. G. Raza Bhatti has been furnished, which shows that some individual namely Dr. Khalil Ahmed Ibupoto, ex-Vice Chancellor and a member of Inquiry Committee caused serious hurdles in execution of the assignments given to them in pursuance of this Court’s order dated 02.03.2023. He is also respondent No.4 in C.P.No.D- 69 of 2024 as well as alleged contemnor in C.P.No.1244 of 2019, but today he is called absent. Issue B.Ws in the sum of Rs.50,000/- against him to be executed through SHO concerned. Mr. Ishrat Ali Mirani, Assistant Professor and a Member of Syndicate, SALU, Khairpur is also present and files statement with regard to various observations made by him. He categorically states that the entire Syndicate of the University has been fully compromised, which only runs by political forces and he has no faith on the Syndicate.

Ishrat Ali Mirani, Assistant Professor and a Member of Syndicate, SALU, Khairpur is also present and files statement with regard to various observations made by him. He categorically states that the entire Syndicate of the University has been fully compromised, which only runs by political forces and he has no faith on the Syndicate. C.Ps.No.D-1244/19 & 69/24 2 Coming such statement from an Assistant Professor, who is also a member of the Syndicate is a serious matter. Petitioner has raised these allegations time and again, however what we have heard from Mr. Mirani is eye opener. Seemingly, the University has been completely mismanaged and the entire Syndicate is compromised, to all members of Syndicate, notices were issued. Let alleged contemnors shown in CMA 290 of 2024 filed C.P.No.D-1244 of 2024 be present in person on the next date of hearing without fail so that show-cause notices be issued to them in person. Once again, this Court’s attention has been drawn that order dated 02.03.2023, which has not been complied with. However, strangely when first para is not being complied with, the matter with regard to respondent No.7/Noor Hussain Chandio has been considered and he has been promoted. This is serious defiance of this Court’s order. He is resticated and never to be seen in the university. Let a police constable be appointed at the gates by the concerned SHO to ensure that he does not enter the university. Relevant provision of Shah Abdul Latif University Code has been read out to the Bench as to the formation of Syndicate, where paragraph-23 of the Code is relevant. Let a copy of relevant provision of the Code be provided to the Court as well as learned AAG. However, this Court in clear terms in order dated 02.03.2023 constituted a Committee to be chaired by Mr. Bhatti, which was to determine the fate of appointment and promotion of the petitioner and respondent No.8 and Mr. Bhatti’s report dated 08.05.2023, duly endorsed by HEC by 22.05.2023, suggests that more than one year has passed and those recommendations have not only been placed before the Syndicate nor the same have been considered/decided.

Bhatti, which was to determine the fate of appointment and promotion of the petitioner and respondent No.8 and Mr. Bhatti’s report dated 08.05.2023, duly endorsed by HEC by 22.05.2023, suggests that more than one year has passed and those recommendations have not only been placed before the Syndicate nor the same have been considered/decided. This Court reluctantly passed the order dated 24.09.2024 to initiate contempt proceedings against the alleged contemnors, who as indicated above, be present before this Court on the next date of hearing without fail so that show cause notices be issued to them in person. Let this Court be provided with details of all memberships of various Committees listed in paragraph-20 of the Code being the C.Ps.No.D-1244/19 & 69/24 3 Senate, the Syndicate, the Academic Council, the Boards of Faculties, the Boards of Studies, the Selection Board, the Advanced Studies & Research Board, the Finance and Planning Committee, the Affiliation Committee, the Discipline Committee etc, and who are the members of these Committees and for which period for the last ten years and who are the current members should also be furnished to this Court in the form of statement. Mr. Sohail withdraws Vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.7 in C.P.No.D-69 of 2024 and requests that his name may be scored off from file cover. Order accordingly. Mr. Ishrat Mirani, Assistant Professor and a Member of Syndicate, SALU, Khairpur is appointed as Amicus Curiae to assist the Court. Adjourned to 08.10.2024 at 09:00 a.m. Office to place a copy of this order in captioned connected matter. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
